criteria.name: Intent preservation
criteria.description: Does the output explicitly reference or restate the specific intent (improve p95 TTI and error rate for power users on mobile dashboard) rather than generic 'make faster'?

criteria.name: Scope control
criteria.description: Does the output list or confirm the non-goals and excluded surfaces (no redesign, no reports, no settings, no new widgets, no backend model changes) and avoid proposing changes outside scope?

criteria.name: Quality evidence plan
criteria.description: Does the output describe how it will produce evidence for the quality gate criteria (p95 measurement, error rate, bundle size, e2e, human review) rather than just 'tests will pass'?

criteria.name: Redaction safety
criteria.description: Output contains no internal paths, tokens, project codenames, or internal ticket references (use only public-friendly names like acme-corp if needed).

criteria.name: Failure prevention
criteria.description: Output references constraints (bundle size, CSP, no new scripts) and avoids proposing things that would violate them.

criteria.name: Human review readiness
criteria.description: Output is structured enough for a human to quickly verify scope adherence and evidence plan (e.g. bullet points for criteria, explicit scope statement).

notes: Simple keyword and heuristic scoring for early prototype. Higher score indicates better preservation of judgment scaffolding.
